en: Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.
zh: 信就是對所盼望的事的把握，是還沒有看見的事的明證。

en: For by it the people of old received their commendation.
zh: 因著這信心，古人得到了稱許。

en: By faith we understand that the universe was created by the word of God, so that what is seen was not made out of things that are visible.
zh: 因著信，我們就明白宇宙 是因著 神的話造成的。這樣，那看得見的就是從那看不見的造出來的。

en: By faith Abel offered to God a more acceptable sacrifice than Cain, through which he was commended as righteous, God commending him by accepting his gifts. And through his faith, though he died, he still speaks.
zh: 因著信，亞伯比該隱獻上更美的祭品給 神；藉著這信心，他被 神稱許為義人，這是 神指著他的禮物所作的見證；他雖然死了，卻藉著信仍然說話。

en: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he should not see death, and he was not found, because God had taken him. Now before he was taken he was commended as having pleased God.
zh: 因著信，以諾被遷去了，使他不至於死，人也找不著他，因為 神把他遷去了。原來在遷去以前，他已經得了 神喜悅他的明證。

en: And without faith it is impossible to please him, for whoever would draw near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who seek him.
zh: 沒有信，就不能得到 神的喜悅；因為來到 神面前的人，必須信 神存在，並且信他會賞賜那些尋求他的人。

en: By faith Noah, being warned by God concerning events as yet unseen, in reverent fear constructed an ark for the saving of his household. By this he condemned the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes by faith.
zh: 因著信，挪亞在還沒有看見的事上，得了 神的警告，就動了敬畏的心，做了一艘方舟，使他全家得救。藉著這信心，他就定了那世代的罪，自己也承受了那因信而來的義。

en: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was to receive as an inheritance. And he went out, not knowing where he was going.
zh: 因著信，亞伯拉罕在蒙召的時候，就聽命往他將要承受為業的地方去；他出去的時候，還不知道要往哪裡去。

en: By faith he went to live in the land of promise, as in a foreign land, living in tents with Isaac and Jacob, heirs with him of the same promise.
zh: 因著信，他在應許之地寄居，好像是在異鄉，與承受同樣應許的以撒、雅各一樣住在帳棚裡。

en: For he was looking forward to the city that has foundations, whose designer and builder is God.
zh: 因為他等待那座有根基的城，就是 神所設計所建造的。

en: By faith Sarah herself received power to conceive, even when she was past the age, since she considered him faithful who had promised.
zh: 因著信，甚至撒拉，她雖然過了生育的年齡，還是能夠懷孕，因為她認為那應許她的是信實的。

en: Therefore from one man, and him as good as dead, were born descendants as many as the stars of heaven and as many as the innumerable grains of sand by the seashore.
zh: 所以從一個好像已死的人，竟然生出許多子孫來，仿佛天上的星那麼眾多，海邊的沙那麼無數。

en: These all died in faith, not having received the things promised, but having seen them and greeted them from afar, and having acknowledged that they were strangers and exiles on the earth.
zh: 這些人都是存著信心死了的，還沒有得著所應許的，只不過是從遠處看見，就表示歡迎，又承認他們在世上是異鄉人，是客旅。

en: For people who speak thus make it clear that they are seeking a homeland.
zh: 因為說這樣話的人，是表明他們在尋求一個家鄉。

en: If they had been thinking of that land from which they had gone out, they would have had opportunity to return.
zh: 如果他們懷念已經離開了的地方，還有可以回去的機會。

en: But as it is, they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed to be called their God, for he has prepared for them a city.
zh: 但是現在他們所嚮往的，是一個更美的、在天上的家鄉。所以， 神不以他們稱他為 神而覺得羞恥；因為他已經為他們預備了一座城。

en: By faith Abraham, when he was tested, offered up Isaac, and he who had received the promises was in the act of offering up his only son,
zh: 因著信，亞伯拉罕在受試驗的時候，就把以撒獻上；這就是那歡喜領受應許的人，獻上了自己的獨生子；

en: of whom it was said, "Through Isaac shall your offspring be named."
zh: 論到這個兒子，曾經有話說：“以撒生的，才可以稱為你的後裔。”

en: He considered that God was able even to raise him from the dead, from which, figuratively speaking, he did receive him back.
zh: 亞伯拉罕認定， 神能使人從死人中復活，因此，就喻意說，他的確從死裡得回他的兒子。

en: By faith Isaac invoked future blessings on Jacob and Esau.
zh: 因著信，以撒給雅各和以掃祝福，論到將來的事。

en: By faith Jacob, when dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph, bowing in worship over the head of his staff.
zh: 因著信，雅各臨死的時候，分別為約瑟的兒子祝福，又倚著杖頭敬拜 神。

en: By faith Joseph, at the end of his life, made mention of the exodus of the Israelites and gave directions concerning his bones.
zh: 因著信，約瑟臨終的時候，提到以色列子民出埃及的事，並且為自己的骸骨留下遺言。

en: By faith Moses, when he was born, was hidden for three months by his parents, because they saw that the child was beautiful, and they were not afraid of the king's edict.
zh: 因著信，摩西的父母在摩西生下來以後，因為看見孩子俊美，就把他藏了三個月，不怕王的命令。

en: By faith Moses, when he was grown up, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter,
zh: 因著信，摩西長大了以後，就拒絕被稱為法老女兒的兒子。

en: choosing rather to be mistreated with the people of God than to enjoy the fleeting pleasures of sin.
zh: 他寧願選擇和 神的子民一同受苦，也不肯享受罪惡中暫時的快樂。

en: He considered the reproach of Christ greater wealth than the treasures of Egypt, for he was looking to the reward.
zh: 在他看來，為著基督受的凌辱，比埃及的財物更寶貴，因為他注視將來的賞賜。

en: By faith he left Egypt, not being afraid of the anger of the king, for he endured as seeing him who is invisible.
zh: 因著信，他離開了埃及，不怕王的忿怒；因為他堅定不移，就像看見了人不能看見的 神。

en: By faith he kept the Passover and sprinkled the blood, so that the Destroyer of the firstborn might not touch them.
zh: 因著信，他立了逾越節和灑血的禮，免得那滅命的侵犯以色列人的長子。

en: By faith the people crossed the Red Sea as if on dry land, but the Egyptians, when they attempted to do the same, were drowned.
zh: 因著信，他們走過了紅海，好像走過旱地一樣；埃及人也試著要過去，就被淹沒了。

en: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down after they had been encircled for seven days.
zh: 因著信，耶利哥的城牆被圍繞了七天，就倒塌了。

en: By faith Rahab the prostitute did not perish with those who were disobedient, because she had given a friendly welcome to the spies.
zh: 因著信，妓女喇合和和平平接待了偵察的人，就沒有和那些不順從的人一起滅亡。

en: And what more shall I say? For time would fail me to tell of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, of David and Samuel and the prophets-
zh: 我還要再說甚麼呢？如果再要述說基甸、巴拉、參孫、耶弗他、大衛、撒母耳和眾先知的事，時間就不夠了。

en: who through faith conquered kingdoms, enforced justice, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,
zh: 他們藉著信，就戰勝了敵國，伸張了正義，得到了應許，堵住了獅子的口，

en: quenched the power of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, were made strong out of weakness, became mighty in war, put foreign armies to flight.
zh: 消滅了烈火的威力，逃脫了刀劍的鋒刃，軟弱變成剛強，在戰爭中顯出大能，把外國的軍隊擊退。

en: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Some were tortured, refusing to accept release, so that they might rise again to a better life.
zh: 有些婦女得回從死裡復活的親人；但也有些人忍受了酷刑，不肯接受釋放，為的是要得著更美的復活。

en: Others suffered mocking and flogging, and even chains and imprisonment.
zh: 又有些人遭受了戲弄、鞭打，甚至捆鎖、監禁；

en: They were stoned, they were sawn in two, they were killed with the sword. They went about in skins of sheep and goats, destitute, afflicted, mistreated-
zh: 被石頭打死，被鋸鋸死， 被刀殺死。他們披著綿羊山羊的皮到處奔跑、受窮乏、遭患難、被虐待；

en: of whom the world was not worthy- wandering about in deserts and mountains, and in dens and caves of the earth.
zh: 原是這世界不配有的人。他們飄流無定，在曠野、山嶺、石洞和地穴棲身。

en: And all these, though commended through their faith, did not receive what was promised,
zh: 所有這些人都藉著信得了稱許，卻還沒有得著所應許的；

en: since God had provided something better for us, that apart from us they should not be made perfect.
zh: 因為 神已經為我們預備了更美的事，使他們若不跟我們在一起，就不能完全。
